project.name: walk-to-nyc
project.description: Shared walking/biking accountability tracker for two friends preparing for a NYC trip
project.namespace_note: SQLite uses a single file — the file IS the project boundary. No schema namespace needed.

views.name: user_progress
views.purpose: Main dashboard query — everything needed to render one user's panel
views.query: SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.name, u.emoji_descriptor, u.route_origin_address, u.route_total_miles, u.seed_miles, COALESCE(SUM(de.miles), 0) AS logged_miles, u.seed_miles + COALESCE(SUM(de.miles), 0) AS total_miles, CASE WHEN u.route_total_miles > 0 THEN ROUND((u.seed_miles + COALESCE(SUM(de.miles), 0)) / u.route_total_miles * 100, 1) ELSE 0 END AS percent_complete, COALESCE(COUNT(CASE WHEN de.miles > 0 THEN 1 END), 0) AS days_active, MAX(de.date) AS last_entry_date FROM user u LEFT JOIN daily_entry de ON u.id = de.user_id WHERE u.role = 'walker' GROUP BY u.id

views.name: waypoints_passed
views.purpose: Which waypoints has a user reached, based on total miles
views.query: SELECT w.user_id, w.name, w.mile_marker, w.display_order, CASE WHEN (u.seed_miles + COALESCE(logged.total, 0)) >= w.mile_marker THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS passed FROM waypoint w JOIN user u ON w.user_id = u.id LEFT JOIN (SELECT user_id, SUM(miles) AS total FROM daily_entry GROUP BY user_id) logged ON w.user_id = logged.user_id WHERE w.selected = 1 ORDER BY w.user_id, w.display_order

views.name: recent_entries
views.purpose: Entries within the 7-day edit window for typo correction
views.query: SELECT id, user_id, date, miles, CASE WHEN date >= date('now', '-7 days') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS editable FROM daily_entry ORDER BY date DESC

functions.name: log_miles
functions.purpose: Record miles for a specific date (core write operation)
functions.behavior: Validate: user exists, role=walker, setup_complete=1, date not future, miles >= 0, no existing entry for that date. INSERT into daily_entry.

functions.name: edit_entry
functions.purpose: Correct a typo on a recent entry (7-day window)
functions.behavior: Validate: entry exists, belongs to requesting user, date within 7 days of today, new_miles >= 0. UPDATE miles and updated_at.

functions.name: complete_setup
functions.purpose: One-time route setup after maps API call
functions.behavior: Validate: user exists, setup_complete=0. UPDATE user with route fields, seed_miles, setup_complete=1. INSERT all waypoints. Atomic transaction.

functions.name: regenerate_token
functions.purpose: Admin kills old token and issues new one
functions.behavior: Validate: requesting user is admin. Generate new random token, hash it, UPDATE user.token_hash. Return raw token.

functions.name: get_gap_days
functions.purpose: Return list of dates needing gap-fill entries
functions.behavior: Find MAX(date) from daily_entry for user. Return all dates from day-after-last-entry through yesterday. Empty array if no gaps.

functions.name: generate_final_report
functions.returns: Report object: total_miles, percent_complete, days_active, total_entry_days, longest_streak, waypoints_passed (list), first_entry_date, last_entry_date
functions.behavior: Query user_progress view + all daily_entries ordered by date. Compute longest consecutive streak (days with miles > 0). List waypoints where total_miles >= mile_marker.

security.note: SQLite has no RLS or database roles. All access control enforced in app middleware.

authentication.flow: Token in URL param on first visit → validated → long-lived cookie set (expires Oct 2026) → return visits use cookie
authentication.token_spec: 32 bytes cryptographically random, URL-safe base64 encoded
authentication.storage: Only token_hash stored in database. Raw token exists only in user's link and cookie.
authentication.regeneration: Admin generates new token → old hash overwritten → old link/cookie instantly invalid

rules.can_read: Own progress + friend's progress (read-only) + own recent entries
rules.can_write: Own daily entries (log + edit within 7 days) + own setup
rules.restrictions: Must complete setup before logging. Cannot access admin functions.

rules.can_write: Regenerate tokens. Manual data fixes if needed.
rules.restrictions: Cannot log miles or run setup flow. No data entry footprint.

security.transport: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare + fly.io. Tokens never travel in plaintext.

agentReadiness.rationale: Pure traditional architecture. 3 human consumers, deterministic flows, no agent interaction needed.

rationale.why: 3 users, 3 tables, zero real-time needs, defined lifespan. Single file = simplest archive story. No separate DB service needed. Embedded in the app container.
alternatives: Supabase — overkill for 3 users, adds namespace overhead for a 7-month app
PocketBase — good fit but abstracts away the auth and API work that serves the learning goal

rationale.why: 3 known users, low sensitivity data, defined lifespan. Tokens live for the app's life. Cookie set on first visit avoids repeated link lookups. Simplest auth that works.
alternatives: PocketBase/Supabase built-in auth — heavier than needed for 3 static users
Username/password — unnecessary complexity, more to forget/break

rationale.chosen: Store all API candidates with selected flag
rationale.why: Avoids re-calling the maps API if a user wants to change their waypoint selections later. Selected flag filters at query time.
alternatives: Store only selected waypoints — simpler but requires new API call to change selections

rationale.chosen: Field on user table, not a daily_entry row
rationale.why: Seed miles aren't tied to a date. Keeping them separate avoids a fake 'day zero' entry that would skew streak calculations and day counts.
alternatives: Special daily_entry with a sentinel date — pollutes the entries table, complicates queries

rationale.why: SQLite has no RLS or database roles. With 3 users and app-layer middleware, this is the natural and only approach. Threat model is minimal — worst case is fake mile logging.
alternatives: Move to Postgres/Supabase for RLS — unnecessary infrastructure for the threat model

rationale.why: Pure procedure — log a number, display a number, compare two numbers. No judgment, no NLP, no adaptive behavior. Traditional request-response is correct.
alternatives: Any agent involvement — would add complexity with zero value for deterministic CRUD flows

seedData.note: Tokens generated at deploy time. Waypoints populated per-user during setup flow via maps API.

lifecycle.deploy: Fly.io single machine, walk.haugaard.app via Cloudflare DNS (gray cloud)
teardown: Generate final reports for each walker
Save final reports to VPS alongside database
